resources.string: Notice yourself, one week at a time
At 5:00 every %1$s, the completed reflection and six-course round generate the previous week’s pressure record.
No pressure score was generated because the previous round’s reflection was not completed
Each point is one completed week. The six-course round and reflection are combined.
Your trend starts after the first weekly reflection.
Complete one weekly reflection and work through all six guided mindfulness courses.
A new round starts every %1$s at 5:00. Complete the six courses in any order.
All six courses are complete for this round.
Every %1$s, review sleep, attention, relationships, surroundings and physical signals across the past week.
The reflection is available on %1$s only.
All six guided audio courses are stored on your device. A full play counts toward this round, and you can replay any course.
Audio provided by the NSW Multicultural Health Communication Service; the English interface plays the English recordings.
Use the five senses to return to the present and reconnect body, mind, and surroundings.
Notice breath and body, nurture helpful thoughts, and let the mind gradually clear.
Move attention through the body, noticing sensations without judgment and easing tension.
Watch thoughts arrive, remain, and pass while practising a different way to respond.
Offer care and kind wishes to yourself, loved ones, and people you find difficult.
Make room for painful emotions and practise understanding and responding to them helpfully.
There are no perfect answers. Choose what most closely reflects the past seven days.
At 5:00 on %1$s, your answers and six-course completion will generate this round’s pressure score.

string-array.name: assessment_questions
string-array.item: How would you describe your sleep quality over the past week?
Did you pick up your phone and switch repeatedly between apps?
How did you respond to different views or unexpected change?
Did a significant stressful event happen this week?
How were your surroundings and personal care?
How was your work or study state?
How did contact with family and friends feel?
Did you experience persistent tension, headaches, palpitations, or appetite changes?
Could you make time for interests, movement, or simple rest?
Could you stop and rest despite unfinished tasks?

string-array.name: assessment_sleep_options
string-array.item: Urgent change; daily life is affected

string-array.name: assessment_views_options
string-array.item: Strongly resistant and kept replaying it

string-array.name: assessment_environment_options
string-array.item: Often lacked energy to care for things

string-array.name: assessment_work_options
string-array.item: Could hardly begin or sustain basics

string-array.name: assessment_relationship_options
string-array.item: Reduced contact and sometimes avoided it
Clearly avoided contact or felt isolated

string-array.name: assessment_recovery_options
string-array.item: Could not stop or lost interest

string-array.name: assessment_worry_options
string-array.item: Could accept unfinished work and rest
Sometimes thought about it, then let go
Often replayed it and struggled to stop
Persistent worry made rest nearly impossible

resources.string: The current screen will be pinned. Leaving the app interrupts this record.
Guided audio stored on this device
Course complete and added to this round.
You left the app, so this practice was not completed.
Practice ended early and does not count toward this round.
Choose the weekly rhythm that suits you; records and audio still stay on this device.
At 5:00 every %1$s, the previous round’s pressure is generated and a new round begins. The reflection opens the day before (%2$s).
If the reflection is still incomplete at 10:00 on %1$s, the app sends one reminder.
Only used for the incomplete-reflection reminder on %1$s. No usage or location access.
Used for the interface and guided audio
Controls the app’s light and dark display
All records and audio remain on this device.
NeuroTrack supports self-observation and mindfulness only. It does not provide medical diagnosis or treatment. Seek professional help if distress persists or you may harm yourself.
Only reminds you at 10:00 on %1$s when the weekly reflection is incomplete.
This week is worth a brief look back
This round’s reflection is still incomplete. Take a few minutes to check in with the week.
